item.title: NHS data chief pushes for deeper rollout of Palantir technology despite outcry
item.description: Ming Tang says the platform, which collates patient information in one place, is delivering ‘outstanding results’ in England

item.title: SEC names Gibson Dunn partner David Woodcock to run enforcement unit
item.description: Appointment comes as Wall Street watchdog accelerates business-friendly deregulation drive

item.title: Go to court and lose out on £9bn car finance redress scheme, says FCA boss
item.description: Comments by Nikhil Rathi position watchdog for stand-off with claims management companies and law firms

item.title: SEC chair says US states should lead on policing corporate behaviour
item.description: Comments by Paul Atkins come as Trump administration is seeking to loosen regulatory constraints

item.title: Haircut for taxpayers after lenders take control of struggling broadband provider
item.description: National Wealth Fund gave Gigaclear £240mn guarantee three years ago and is now the biggest shareholder

item.title: Our quest not to solve bitcoin’s great mystery
item.description: Because what is this, 2014?

item.title: The UK needs to make choices on tech strategy
item.description: As with Brexit, public anger could swing behind the idea that we need to take back control

item.title: Why the ‘SaaSpocalypse’ doomsayers are wrong
item.description: Every major tech platform shift follows the same pattern: short-term pain for long-term gain

item.title: Meta releases first AI model since Zuckerberg’s spending spree
item.description: Muse Spark ‘purpose-built’ for social media apps as investors question huge AI investment

item.title: How this oil shock unwinds
item.description: At a rate of 1mn barrels a day of overproduction, it would take not far off two years to make up the buffers

item.title: Billionaire son of ‘world’s richest banker’ challenges $35mn legal fees
item.description: Alberto Safra was charged more than $162,000 on one day alone by US law firm WilmerHale

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s vital East-West oil pipeline attacked
item.description: Conduit has become an economic lifeline for the kingdom since the near closure of the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Ian Cheshire named new Ofcom chair
item.description: Former Kingfisher chief is the government’s choice to replace Lord Michael Grade at the media regulator

item.title: Exxon warns of $6.5bn hit from Iran war as hedging timing masks gains
item.description: US oil group says bulk of impact on first-quarter earnings is accounting issue that will be offset as contracts complete

item.title: Extreme weather is already costing global businesses
item.description: Rio Tinto’s $800mn revenue hit highlights growing costs of business interruption

item.title: The new super-prime dilemma: sell, rent or manage?
item.description: For those who have moved their primary residence away from London, a sluggish sales market is prompting them to weigh up their options

item.title: How I became a meme in Nigeria
item.description: My moment of internet fame reflects the country’s sceptical political mood

item.title: Delta to cut routes and raise charges to counter $2bn jet fuel hit
item.description: US airline faces higher bill from soaring prices driven by Iran war

item.title: Hedge funds suffered worst losses since Covid in Iran war turmoil
item.description: Managers have struggled to navigate huge market swings sparked by conflict

item.title: Toyota keeps hydrogen as a hedge
item.description: Retaining flexibility may prove more valuable than going all in too early on a single bet

item.title: UK house prices fall in March as uncertainty over Middle East war weighs on demand
item.description: Halifax reports market slowdown as mortgage rates rise

item.title: CK Hutchison takes Maersk to arbitration over Panama port
item.description: Hong Kong conglomerate escalates legal campaign over its ejection from canal terminals

item.title: Shell’s oil traders get earnings boost from Iran war
item.description: FTSE 100 group expects a drop in gas production because of hit to facilities in Qatar

item.title: US and Iran agree 2-week ceasefire that will open Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Agreement sparks relief rally in global stock markets as oil price tumbles

item.title: Aldermore Bank put up for sale after owner attacks UK car finance redress scheme
item.description: South Africa’s FirstRand says industry-wide £9.1bn programme is ‘deeply flawed’

item.title: Russian military hackers reroute British internet users’ traffic
item.description: UK National Cyber Security Centre warns vulnerable routers will let attackers steal passwords and log-in details

item.title: Earnings expectations are too high
item.description: Plus AI and jobs

item.title: FirstFT: Trump and Iran announce 2-week ceasefire
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Turkey’s gold sales and French miner Eramet

item.title: Private equity’s secondaries magic
item.description: Last year investors ploughed a record $166bn into the funds backing ageing assets

item.title: Long Covid to cost OECD economies up to $135bn a year
item.description: New research predicts impact of lower productivity and workers quitting because of long-term illness after Covid-19
